result.q3_skip_reason: Task premise was incorrect: lethal_retrieve is not confined to the retired script. It is a live function defined at src/polaris_graph/wiki/mesh/retrieve/lethal.py:95 and imported by two ACTIVE test files (tests/integration/test_mesh_e2e.py:31 and tests/unit/test_mesh_lethal_retrieve.py:44) in addition to scripts/_retired_2026_06_14/pg_mesh_preflight.py:28. The name is also embedded in a persisted test filename (test_mesh_lethal_retrieve.py) and appears throughout .codex audit logs as a fixture path. This fails codex rename condition (b) (must NOT be referenced as an import / fixture path / persisted label elsewhere), so per the codex decision it is SKIPPED rather than renamed. Renaming would require touching two live test files and renaming a test file, well beyond the stated 'retired dir, low risk' scope.

Q2 (PG_GENERATOR_MODEL '' standardization) is byte-safe ONLY in the deployed, env-dominated path where PG_GENERATOR_MODEL=glm-5.2. Codex declined to certify it byte-safe overall because the unset-env path (CI, tests, dev shells) is not proven safe.

The oracle SHA (9c0a3d...db98) establishes byte-identity for the env-dominated path only; it does NOT establish coverage of the unset-env fallback path. So 'oracle_matches:true' does not close the gap codex flagged.

Root gap: the prior code fallback value was described only as 'whatever-it-was' / 'previously-nonempty default'. Replacing an unknown prior nonempty default with '' could silently change model selection or trip downstream validation in any path that leaves the env unset. This is the crux of the REVISE.

Local verification note: PG_GENERATOR_MODEL does not appear anywhere in the current working tree (grep of *.py returns nothing, git diff/stash empty). The described Q2/Q3 edits are not present in /home/polaris/polaris_project locally -- this gate ran against the reported evidence, not a local diff.

Recommended remediation before re-gating Q2: identify the exact PRIOR fallback string at each of the 4 sites and confirm it was already '' (making the change a true no-op) OR add an unset-env test/oracle proving downstream behavior is unchanged when PG_GENERATOR_MODEL is absent.
